title: Session + claim lib: O_EXCL claims with TTL/heartbeat (RED-first)
action: STEP 0 (gating, validating W4): run the win32 half of the feasibility probe before any source edit — copy .bee/spikes/fresh-session-handoff/probe_atomic_claim.mjs to the Windows temp dir (get it via cmd.exe /c echo %TEMP%, convert with wslpath; Windows Node exists at /mnt/c/Program Files/nodejs/node.exe, v22.14.0 verified) and run it with Windows Node using a Windows-style path (critical pattern 20260708). FAIL → return [BLOCKED] citing the probe output; the slice goes back to planning. PASS → record the output line in the trace and proceed. THEN build templates/lib/claims.mjs: session records (.bee/sessions/<session-id>.json: id, started_at, last_heartbeat) and per-cell claim records (.bee/claims/<cell-id>.json) created atomically with exclusive-create ('wx'). Typed-failure contract (pinned, validating repair): mutating APIs return {ok:true, ...} | {ok:false, code, reason} — codes like CLAIMED/GATE_HELD/NOT_OWNER — and never throw for contention (follow reservations.mjs's result-object convention); fsh-2 asserts this exact shape. API: createSession/readSession/heartbeatSession; claimCellFile(sessionId, cellId, ttl) — one winner via 'wx'; releaseClaim; adoptClaim(cellId, newSessionId) — ADOPTION DESIGN (pinned, panel W2): acquire an exclusive-create gate file (<cellId>.adopting, 'wx'), rewrite the owner IN PLACE so the claim file never disappears (concurrent 'wx' claimers keep getting EEXIST), then remove the gate; NEVER bare read-modify-writeJsonAtomic, NEVER release-then-reclaim. sweepExpiredClaims (pinned, panel W3): acquire the same per-claim gate, RE-VERIFY under the gate that TTL is expired AND heartbeat is stale (pattern 20260710), only then reclaim; gate held → skip, never wait. isClaimActive reuses reservations.mjs TTL semantics. All record paths repo-relative under .bee/ — network-filesystem project dirs (NFS mounts) are documented as unsupported (O_EXCL caveat). RED-first: add failing suite rows to test_lib.mjs before implementing; keep RED evidence in the trace. Vendoring of .bee/bin/lib is owned by self-onboard at release, not this cell (panel note). S1 boundary: no consumer wiring. NOTE: single-process rows here prove post-states and the typed contract; the concurrency windows themselves are proven by fsh-2 — S1 caps as a unit, fsh-1 alone does not represent the no-dual-owner guarantee (panel W1).

truths: Windows-side probe PASS line is recorded in the trace before any source edit (or the cell is [BLOCKED], never worked around)
Two sequential claim attempts for one cell id: first wins, second receives the typed {ok:false} contention result — no throw
A claim whose TTL expired but whose heartbeat is fresh is NOT reclaimable; TTL expired AND heartbeat stale (re-verified under the claim's gate) is
After adoptClaim the old session no longer owns and the new one does, and the claim file existed continuously throughout (no observable deletion window in post-state checks)

artifacts.substantive: exports the full session+claim API with the pinned typed-failure contract and gate-based adopt/sweep, no TODO stubs

key_links: test_lib.mjs exercises claims.mjs directly with RED-first rows
prohibitions: No edits to state.mjs, cells.mjs, guards.mjs, inject.mjs, bee.mjs, command-registry.mjs, or any hooks/ file
No absolute or system-temp path in any claim/session record
No bare read-modify-write of a claim file outside its exclusive gate

trace.outcome: Session + claim lib shipped: templates/lib/claims.mjs exports createSession/readSession/heartbeatSession, claimCellFile ('wx' one-winner, typed CLAIMED naming holder+expiry), releaseClaim (owner-only, under gate), adoptClaim (exclusive <cellId>.adopting gate, owner rewritten IN PLACE via atomic rename — claim file never disappears), sweepExpiredClaims (gate + re-verify TTL expired AND heartbeat stale, gate held => skip), isClaimActive (reservations TTL semantics). Typed contract {ok:true,...}|{ok:false,code,reason} — never throws for contention. Records repo-relative under .bee/; NFS documented unsupported in the module header. STEP 0 win32 probe PASS recorded pre-edit. RED-first: suite failed ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND on claims.mjs before implementation. No consumer wiring (S1 boundary); state.mjs/cells.mjs/guards/inject/bee.mjs/hooks untouched.
deviations: Vendored sibling .bee/bin/lib/claims.mjs created (byte-identical copy of the template): the standing parity test in test_lib.mjs fails the cell's own verify without it. The cell action assigned vendoring to self-onboard at release, but plan.md S1 file bounds explicitly allow 'vendored copy in .bee/bin/lib/ if the parity suite requires it' — it does. Path reserved under Kevin before the copy; deviation rule 3 (blocking issue in the verify path).
